assessor_1_raw:
ASSESSMENT 1:
Three genuine gaps: safety-net unvalidated extension, capacity-driven threshold without cost-benefit validation, HRRP penalty exposure unanalyzed. All addressable with structured validation. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
assessor_2_raw:
ASSESSMENT 2:
Safety-net distribution shift creates unquantified clinical risk in the planned six-month extension. Threshold selection methodology is incomplete given the known cost asymmetry. HRRP analysis is feasible pre-launch. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
assessor_3_raw:
ASSESSMENT 3:
All three concerns are deployment-readiness issues rather than model invalidations. The model may be appropriate for the existing hospitals but requires pre-specified validation conditions for the safety-net extension. Threshold cost-benefit and HRRP analyses are pre-launch requirements. Verdict: EMPIRICAL_TEST_AGREED.
adjudication_raw: Unanimous empirical_test_agreed. Three issues confirmed. Empirical test specification covers the safety-net performance and threshold validation questions.

empirical_test.measure: Prospective AUC-ROC at safety-net hospital during six-month pre-extension window; break-even analysis at current threshold ($45 cost vs. $12,000 readmission cost); HRRP diagnosis-group stratified performance in holdout data
empirical_test.success_criterion: Safety-net AUC-ROC within 8 percentage points of existing-hospital performance; threshold precision above cost-neutral break-even; no systematic HRRP diagnosis group under-performance
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Safety-net AUC-ROC degrades > 10 pp; threshold precision below break-even; HRRP diagnosis groups show > 15% relative recall gap vs. overall model
